message:
fix(doctor): use typeof() instead of IS NULL for NULL detection

The partial index `WHERE actor != ''` causes SQLite's query planner to
skip NULL rows when using IS NULL predicates, returning 0 even when NULLs
exist. Use typeof(column) = 'null' to bypass the index and detect the
actual storage class. Also fix the remediation SQL to use the same
typeof() pattern.

See issue #177 for details on the partial index behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
